subject: grammar
title: Conditionals: Dialogue Proofreading
instruction: Decide if the underlined verbs ([1], [2], etc.) in the dialogue are correct or not. Type '✓' if they are right and type the correct version if they are wrong.
passage:
Teacher: Is it better to have one special friend or lots of good ones?
Student: I think that if you have lots of friends, you 1 [1]. However, I feel that everyone should have someone special. If you 2 [2], you won't have someone to talk to at difficult times in your life.
Teacher: When do people make most friends?
Student: Well, everyone makes friends when they are at school. If you 3 [3] where everyone is your own age, you 4 [4].
Teacher: Do you think that friends need to be similar ages?
Student: Well, generally friends from school are similar ages. But when you 5 [5], for example, you meet people of different ages. If you get on well with someone and you 6 [6], then age 7 [7].
Teacher: Do people need to have things in common to be friends?
Student: Well, yes. If you 8 [8], you will probably get on well. But having said that, I have a very good friend who is completely different from me. She loves sport and I hate it. If you asked me why we were friends, I 9 [9]! Maybe it's just chance - if you are in a certain place at a certain time you 10 [10], but if you 11 [11] at a different time in a different place it 12 [12].
Teacher: What different roles do friends play in people's lives?
Student: Well, your friends are the people you choose to be with. And if you 13 [13], you often turn to your friends. It works the other way too. When your friends need you, you 14 [14].

questions.text: 6. will have a lot in common
